wins: Landed a checkpoint-note contract in aoa-skills without widening the existing explicit-only session-growth ring.
Landed checkpoint-aware detect, append, status, and promote flows in aoa-sdk with reviewed closeout linkage.
Landed a lightweight Dionysus promoted-note sink so mid-session reviewed notes no longer need the heavy session-harvest family by default.
frictions: The route still needs more non-implementation repetitions before automation pressure becomes honest.
aoa-sdk needed one bounded mypy-tail repair after GitHub CI, which shows the lane is real but still young.
v1 intentionally avoids git hooks and background daemons, so checkpoint capture still depends on explicit command use.

extracts.title: Checkpoint-Aware Session Growth Route
extracts.summary: A multi-step route for capturing meaningful checkpoints into a local note, reviewing density, promoting that note when warranted, and only then handing off to heavier closeout or Dionysus lineage surfaces.
notes: The route spans three repositories and preserves distinct authority layers rather than collapsing into one leaf skill.

extracts.title: Detect-Capture-Promote Boundary Law
extracts.summary: A reusable workflow law that keeps checkpoint detection read-only, note capture append-only, and promotion explicit so lower-authority growth surfaces do not impersonate harvest verdicts.
notes: The implementation lives in aoa-sdk, but the enduring value is broader workflow discipline about authority boundaries.

automation_candidate.title: Checkpoint-Aware Session Growth Loop
automation_candidate.current_manual_route: meaningful checkpoint -> aoa surfaces detect --phase checkpoint -> aoa checkpoint append -> aoa checkpoint status -> reviewed promote -> closeout handoff or Dionysus promoted note

deferrals.title: Automation-ready promotion
deferrals.reason: The route is now named and landed, but it still needs reviewed non-implementation repeats before honest automation pressure exists.

deferrals.title: Quest promotion
deferrals.reason: This was the first implementation-and-landing pass, not a re-earned repeated quest loop.

deferrals.title: Implicit hooks
deferrals.reason: v1 intentionally keeps checkpoint capture command-driven rather than hook-driven or daemon-driven.

quest_hooks.reason: The repeated unit is emerging, but donor harvest and progression still preserve the truth better than final promotion authority.
